title: Robert De Niro Has Played A Vigilante, A Mob Boss, AND A Guy Who Ruins Thanksgiving — Which One Are You?
dek: From Travis Bickle's quiet rage to Jack Byrnes' full-blown interrogations, De Niro's filmography has basically covered every personality on Earth — find out which one is secretly you.

quiz.intro: No wrong answers here — just answer honestly and let the chaos sort itself out.

questions.question: How do you actually handle conflict?

answers.text: I stew on it alone until it boils over completely.
I stay calm and settle it quietly, on my own terms.
I laugh it off right before I make sure it never happens again.
I interrogate everyone involved until I get the truth.

questions.question: What's your idea of a perfect night?

answers.text: A quiet family dinner where everyone actually respects each other.
Out at the club, buying rounds, holding court.
Running a background check on my daughter's new boyfriend.
Rehearsing my big moment in front of the mirror.

questions.question: Someone disrespects you in public. What's your move?

answers.text: I laugh, buy them a drink, then decide their fate later.
I make a mental note and bring it up again... and again.
I act like it never happened. I'm too busy being a star.
I smile. I've got nothing but time to make it right.

questions.question: What do people constantly misunderstand about you?

answers.text: They think I'm paranoid. I call it thorough.
They think I'm a nobody. I'm one break away from everything.
They think I'm unstable. I'm actually the most patient person they'll ever meet.
They think I'm difficult. I just believe in something bigger than myself.

questions.question: Your family needs you right now. What's your first move?

answers.text: I imagine how great I'll look swooping in to save the day.
Family is exactly the kind of pressure point I know how to use.
I bet on it going well. Literally.
I do the job first. Family comes... eventually.

questions.question: How do you deal with being told no?

answers.text: No just means I've got more time to plan.
I argue loudly, apologize, then bring it up again next week.
I don't ask. I just do what needs doing.
I don't hear no. I hear a challenge.

questions.question: Pick the personality trait you're proudest of.

answers.text: My devotion — to my family, my team, whatever needs believing in.
My reliability. When I say I'll handle it, I handle it.
My loyalty to a cause, even one nobody else can see.
My composure. I never let anyone see me rattled.
Service. I show love by doing the job, no questions asked.
Grand, desperate gestures nobody asked for.
Quiet acts of protection nobody even notices.
Gifts, cash, and a whole lot of charisma.

questions.question: What's your worst quality, by your own admission?

answers.text: I isolate myself until I finally snap.
I keep too much bottled up, even from family.
I trust people right up until I really, really don't.
I don't trust anyone, ever, even when I should.

questions.question: How would your coworkers describe you behind your back?

answers.text: Composed. Respected. You do not want to cross him.
The life of the party — until suddenly he isn't.
Thorough. Suspicious. Slightly terrifying at family gatherings.
Persistent. Delusional, maybe. But he really believes in himself.

results.name: Travis Bickle
results.subtitle: Taxi Driver (1976)
results.description: You're a loner who sees the rot in everything and everyone around you, and it's been quietly building for a while now. You don't do small talk — you do brooding silence punctuated by sudden, unnerving bursts of action that catch everyone off guard.

results.name: Vito Corleone
results.subtitle: The Godfather Part II (1974)
results.description: You lead with quiet composure, not volume, and everyone still knows not to test you. Family is everything, and you protect it with a controlled, almost gentle intensity — real power, for you, is never having to raise your voice.

results.name: Jimmy Conway
results.subtitle: Goodfellas (1990)
results.description: You're the most charismatic person in any room, generous to a fault, always buying the round and cracking the joke. But underneath the charm is a cold calculation — loyal to the people who are loyal to you, and absolutely not to anyone else.

results.name: Jack Byrnes
results.subtitle: Meet the Parents (2000)
results.description: You are constitutionally incapable of taking anyone at their word — you want the receipts, the polygraph, the full background check. It comes from a place of fierce protectiveness, but it means nobody dating into your family is getting an easy pass.

results.name: Rupert Pupkin
results.subtitle: The King of Comedy (1982)
results.description: You are endlessly, unshakably confident that your big break is one conversation away, and you will absolutely not let a little thing like rejection get in the way. You smile through every setback because deep down, you already know you're a star.

results.name: Max Cady
results.subtitle: Cape Fear (1991)
results.description: You're the most patient person anyone's ever met, and that should honestly scare them more than it does. You don't forget a slight, and you're perfectly happy to spend years plotting exactly how to make someone pay for it — all with a disarming smile.

results.name: Pat Solitano Sr.
results.subtitle: Silver Linings Playbook (2012)
results.description: Your love comes wrapped in rituals, superstitions, and a system nobody else quite understands but you swear it works. You love loudly and fight loudly too, but every outburst comes from wanting the people — and the team — you love to win.

results.name: Frank Sheeran
results.subtitle: The Irishman (2019)
results.description: You're the person people call when something needs to get done quietly, reliably, and without a single question asked. You don't show much on the outside, but decades from now you'll still be quietly carrying the weight of every job you ever did.
